hyperedges.label: Collection of system tray icons representing different application states (idle, recording, transcribing) with light and dark theme variants for the Handy speech-to-text desktop app.
Brand logos for the three sponsors (BoltAI, Epicenter, Wordcab) of the Handy cross-platform desktop speech-to-text application.
Core brand identity assets for the Handy application including the primary app icon, full logo, and notification icon, all sharing the same visual design language.
Shared 7-platform build matrix and reusable build delegation
hyperedges.rationale: build-test, main-build, pr-test-build, and release all declare the identical macOS/Ubuntu/Windows x ARM/x64 matrix and delegate to the reusable build.yml workflow, differing mainly in trigger and asset handling.

hyperedges.label: Pre-merge CI validation gates
hyperedges.rationale: These workflows all run on pull_request/push with path filters to validate the codebase (lint+format, Playwright e2e, Rust tests, Nix build) before changes merge.

hyperedges.label: Shared development setup commands (bun install, tauri dev, silero VAD model download)
Floating assistant panel: described in README, logged in PROGRESS, and the assistant Vite window that implements it
Multi-window Vite/Tauri architecture: settings, assistant panel, and recording overlay entry points

nodes.label: Funding Configuration
nodes.rationale: Declares sponsorship channels for the maintainer (GitHub Sponsors, custom donate links, Buy Me a Coffee, Ko-fi).

nodes.label: Bug Report Issue Template
nodes.rationale: Structured bug-report form requesting app version, OS, CPU/GPU, and logs; labeled 'bug' with a '[BUG]' title prefix.

nodes.label: Issue Template Configuration
nodes.rationale: Disables blank issues and routes feature requests, hotkeys, and general questions to GitHub Discussions.

nodes.label: Pull Request Template
nodes.rationale: Mandates duplicate search, a human-written description, community feedback links, testing notes, and AI-assistance disclosure under an active feature freeze.

nodes.label: Build Test Workflow
nodes.rationale: Manually dispatched full cross-platform build that delegates to the reusable build workflow and uploads artifacts.

nodes.label: Reusable Build Workflow
nodes.rationale: Central workflow_call pipeline that compiles the Tauri app across platforms, signs binaries, bundles ONNX Runtime, and uploads installers; invoked by build-test, main-build, pr-test-build, and release.

nodes.label: Code Quality Workflow
nodes.rationale: Runs translation consistency check, ESLint, and Prettier on frontend changes.

nodes.label: Main Branch Build Workflow
nodes.rationale: Runs the full cross-platform build on every push to main so breakage is caught before a manual release.

nodes.label: Nix Build Check Workflow
nodes.rationale: Two-tier Nix CI: quick bun.nix sync + flake eval on any source change, full nix build only when packaging files change.

nodes.label: Playwright Workflow
nodes.rationale: Installs Chromium and runs Playwright end-to-end tests on frontend pull requests, uploading the report on failure.

nodes.label: PR Test Build Workflow
nodes.rationale: Manually builds a specified PR via the reusable build workflow and comments downloadable artifact links back on the PR.

nodes.label: Release Workflow
nodes.rationale: Creates a draft GitHub release from the tauri.conf.json version, then builds and publishes signed binaries to it across all platforms.

nodes.label: Rust Test Workflow
nodes.rationale: Runs cargo test against a mock TranscriptionManager to avoid compiling whisper/Vulkan in CI.

nodes.label: Create Draft Release Job
nodes.rationale: Reads version from tauri.conf.json and creates a draft release via github-script, exposing release-id as an output.

nodes.label: Publish Tauri Job
nodes.rationale: Consumes the create-release release-id and calls the reusable build workflow to attach signed binaries to the draft release.

nodes.label: Comment on PR Job
nodes.rationale: After the build matrix completes, posts a comment with links to the test-build artifacts via github-script.

nodes.label: actions/checkout Action
nodes.rationale: Standard repository checkout action reused across nearly every workflow.

nodes.label: oven-sh/setup-bun Action
nodes.rationale: Installs the Bun runtime used for frontend dependency install and scripts.

nodes.label: swatinem/rust-cache Action
nodes.rationale: Caches the src-tauri Cargo target directory to speed up Rust builds and tests.

nodes.label: dtolnay/rust-toolchain Action
nodes.rationale: Installs stable Rust, adding Apple targets only on macOS runners.

nodes.label: tauri-apps/tauri-action Action
nodes.rationale: Drives the actual Tauri build, signing, and optional release upload with platform-specific GGML/codesigning env vars.

nodes.label: actions/upload-artifact Action
nodes.rationale: Uploads build installers and test reports as retained CI artifacts.

nodes.label: actions/github-script Action
nodes.rationale: Runs inline JS against the GitHub API to create releases and post PR comments.

nodes.label: cachix/install-nix-action Action
nodes.rationale: Installs Nix (nixos-unstable channel) so the flake can be evaluated and built in CI.

nodes.label: Code Signing
nodes.rationale: Apple Developer certificate import/notarization plus Windows trusted-signing-cli, all gated behind the sign-binaries input.

nodes.label: Vulkan SDK Setup
nodes.rationale: Installs the Vulkan SDK across Windows x64/ARM64 and Ubuntu variants for GPU-accelerated whisper inference.

nodes.label: ONNX Runtime Bundling
nodes.rationale: Downloads prebuilt ONNX Runtime and patches tauri.conf.json to bundle the dylib/.so into macOS app and .deb packages.

nodes.label: Cross-Platform Build Matrix
nodes.rationale: Identical 7-target matrix (macOS arm/x64, Ubuntu 22.04/24.04/arm, Windows x64/arm) duplicated across every build-triggering workflow.

nodes.label: GitHub Discussions
nodes.rationale: Designated home for feature requests, hotkey/post-processing ideas, and general questions instead of issues.

nodes.label: Feature Freeze
nodes.rationale: Handy is under a feature freeze; bug fixes are prioritized and new features need community support before a PR is accepted.

nodes.label: AI Assistance Disclosure
nodes.rationale: PRs must disclose whether and how AI tools were used, while still requiring a genuinely human-written description.

nodes.label: Project Funding
nodes.rationale: Sponsorship and donation model supporting the solo maintainer.

nodes.label: Manual Dispatch Trigger
nodes.rationale: workflow_dispatch trigger allowing maintainers to run a workflow manually.

nodes.label: Push to Main Trigger
nodes.rationale: push trigger scoped to the main branch (often path-filtered).

nodes.label: Pull Request Trigger
nodes.rationale: pull_request trigger (usually path-filtered) gating changes before merge.

nodes.label: Handy Architecture Overview
nodes.rationale: Cross-platform desktop speech-to-text built on Tauri 2.x with a Rust backend and a React/TypeScript frontend.

nodes.label: Manager Pattern
nodes.rationale: Core logic is split into Audio, Model, and Transcription managers initialized at startup and shared via Tauri state to keep business logic modular.

nodes.label: Command-Event Architecture
nodes.rationale: Frontend calls backend through Tauri commands; backend pushes updates back through events, giving a clear bidirectional boundary.

nodes.label: Audio Processing Pipeline
nodes.rationale: Audio moves through VAD then Whisper/Parakeet to text then clipboard/paste, isolating each transformation stage.

nodes.label: Single Instance Architecture
nodes.rationale: Relaunching focuses the existing window; remote-control CLI flags work by a second instance forwarding args via tauri_plugin_single_instance then exiting, avoiding duplicate processes.

nodes.label: CLI Parameters
nodes.rationale: CLI flags are runtime-only overrides that never mutate persisted settings, so automation cannot corrupt user config.

nodes.label: Internationalization (i18n)
nodes.rationale: All user-facing strings route through i18next and ESLint forbids hardcoded JSX strings to guarantee translatability.

nodes.label: Technology Stack
nodes.rationale: Core libraries: whisper-rs, cpal, vad-rs, rdev, rubato, rodio.

nodes.label: GitHub Workflow for AI Assistants
nodes.rationale: PR/issue templates are mandatory and feature requests go to Discussions to enforce the feature freeze and keep issues actionable.

nodes.label: Build Prerequisites
nodes.rationale: Requires Rust, Bun, Tauri prerequisites, plus platform-specific toolchains (Xcode CLT, MSVC, ALSA/GTK/Vulkan on Linux).

nodes.label: AppImage Build Troubleshooting
nodes.rationale: linuxdeploy bundles an old strip that fails on newer rolling-release toolchains; deb/rpm/binary bundles still build, so skip AppImage with --bundles deb.

nodes.label: Intel Mac ONNX Runtime Setup
nodes.rationale: No prebuilt ONNX Runtime exists for Intel Macs, so install via Homebrew and link dynamically with ORT_LIB_LOCATION and ORT_PREFER_DYNAMIC_LINK.

nodes.label: CLAUDE.md Redirect
nodes.rationale: Delegates all AI-assistant guidance to AGENTS.md to keep a single source of truth.

nodes.label: Feature Freeze
nodes.rationale: Unrequested new features are rejected and bug fixes prioritized to keep the codebase simple and prevent feature creep.

nodes.label: Forkability Philosophy
nodes.rationale: Aims to be the most forkable STT app, prioritizing simplicity, extensibility, privacy, and accessibility.

nodes.label: AI Assistance Disclosure
nodes.rationale: AI-assisted PRs are welcome but contributors must disclose which tools were used and how extensively, for transparency.

nodes.label: Feature Requests via Discussions
nodes.rationale: Feature ideas go to GitHub Discussions rather than Issues, keeping issues focused on actionable bugs.

nodes.label: Adding a New Language
nodes.rationale: Create an ISO 639-1 folder, copy en/translation.json, translate values only, register in languages.ts, and submit a PR.

nodes.label: Code Style Guidelines
nodes.rationale: Rust uses anyhow errors and Arc<Mutex<T>> shared state; TypeScript/React uses functional components, Zod schemas, and grouped type imports.

nodes.label: ElevenLabs Design Analysis
nodes.rationale: Alpha design-system analysis of the ElevenLabs brand surfaces, captured as tokens for colors, typography, spacing, and components.

nodes.label: Editorial Magazine Aesthetic
nodes.rationale: Brand voltage is photographic not chromatic: off-white canvas, warm near-black ink, no saturated CTA color; atmosphere comes from pastel gradient orbs.

nodes.label: Waldenburg + Inter Typography
nodes.rationale: Waldenburg Light at weight 300 is the editorial display signature; Inter carries body at slightly loose tracking; display is never bolded.

nodes.label: Atmospheric Gradient Orbs
nodes.rationale: Five pastel orbs (mint, peach, lavender, sky, rose) are used only as atmospheric decoration, never as button fills or text colors.

nodes.label: Ink Pill CTA
nodes.rationale: A single near-black pill is the only primary CTA color; no saturated action color is introduced.

nodes.label: Handy Ultra
nodes.rationale: A free local-first voice assistant: local dictation, a floating AI chat panel, screen vision, and spoken answers from one global hotkey.

nodes.label: Local Offline Dictation
nodes.rationale: Whisper/Parakeet run on GPU or CPU and audio never leaves the machine; includes a hands-free F9 toggle.

nodes.label: Floating Assistant Panel
nodes.rationale: An always-on-top glass panel streams LLM answers with markdown, copy, drag, conversation memory, and a collapse-to-pill mode.

nodes.label: Screen Vision
nodes.rationale: A hotkey, spoken intent, or camera button attaches a compressed screenshot of the active monitor to the next message, gated by a master toggle.

nodes.label: Spoken Answers (TTS)
nodes.rationale: A short 1-3 sentence spoken recap is read aloud via Kokoro (local WebGPU), an OpenAI-compatible endpoint, or ElevenLabs.

nodes.label: Local-First Privacy Model
nodes.rationale: Voice-to-text is always local; assistant questions and screenshots go only to the user-configured LLM (which can be local); TTS is local by default; no telemetry or accounts.

nodes.label: Default Hotkeys
nodes.rationale: Ctrl+Space dictate, F9 hands-free, Ctrl+Alt+Space ask assistant, Ctrl+Alt+Shift+Space ask about screen, Ctrl+Alt+A show/hide panel.

nodes.label: OpenAI-Compatible Provider System
nodes.rationale: One provider/key system serves assistant answers, post-processing, and TTS across Azure, OpenAI, Groq, OpenRouter, and local Ollama/LM Studio.

nodes.label: Fork of Handy
nodes.rationale: Handy Ultra forks the original Handy dictation app by CJ Pais, keeping the local STT core and adding assistant, vision, and TTS; MIT licensed.

nodes.label: Project Goal: Replace Paid Voice Assistants
nodes.rationale: Replace Wispr Flow-style subscriptions with a free local-first app: local STT, streaming LLM assistant, screen vision, and spoken summaries.
nodes.norm_label: project goal: replace paid voice assistants

nodes.label: Windows Build Blocker Fixes
nodes.rationale: Set LIBCLANG_PATH for bindgen, redirected cargo target-dir to dodge the >260-char MSBuild FileTracker FTK1011 error, and updated MSVC STL for ort-sys DirectML binaries.

nodes.label: Phase 1: Assistant Mode
nodes.rationale: Added assistant hotkeys, a record-to-LLM pipeline, a third always-on-top Vite panel, and SSE streaming with cache-friendly append-only history.

nodes.label: Azure Image Payload Size Budget
nodes.rationale: After repeated 400 unterminated-string errors, screenshots compress down a ladder to about 48 KB encoded (base64 inflation plus Azure's ~128 KiB JSON-string cap), with HTTP/1.1 forced and history capped on screenshot turns.

nodes.label: Three TTS Engines
nodes.rationale: Kokoro local WebGPU streaming, any OpenAI-compatible /audio/speech endpoint played natively in Rust, and ElevenLabs via API key plus voice ID.

nodes.label: Screen Vision Implementation
nodes.rationale: Captures the monitor under the cursor as a multimodal image_url part, triggered by hotkey, spoken intent, or camera button, with a master privacy toggle.

nodes.label: Roadmap (Phases 2-4)
nodes.rationale: Phase 2 SQLite history and cost display, Phase 3 local llama.cpp sidecar, Phase 4 deployment to a Lunar Lake Zenbook with NPU acceleration.

nodes.label: Main Settings Window
nodes.rationale: Root Vite entry (document titled SpeakoFlow) hosting the main React app via /src/main.tsx.

nodes.label: Assistant Panel Window
nodes.rationale: Transparent Vite entry with a 10px shadow gutter for the always-on-top assistant panel, loading /src/assistant/main.tsx.

nodes.label: Recording Overlay Window
nodes.rationale: Transparent borderless Vite entry for the recording overlay, loading /src/overlay/main.tsx.
